message:
feat(conformance): add setjmp_ops executor stubs (bd-cl5u)

Add execute_* handlers for setjmp_ops family:
- setjmp, _setjmp: stub returns 0 (direct call behavior)
- longjmp: stub (would perform non-local jump)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -477,6 +477,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-cfm1","title":"Printf DISC-001: Implement banker's rounding (round-half-to-even)","description":"Implement IEEE 754 round-half-to-even (banker's rounding) for %.0f and similar precision-limited float formatting. Currently uses round-half-away-from-zero which diverges from glibc.

Reference: sprintf("%.0f", 2.5) should return "2" not "3".
Spec: C11 7.21.6.1 footnote says rounding unspecified but POSIX requires IEEE 754 conformance which specifies round-half-to-even.","acceptance_criteria":"- sprintf("%.0f", 2.5) returns "2"
- sprintf("%.0f", 1.5) returns "2"
- sprintf("%.0f", 3.5) returns "4"
- All printf conformance tests pass including round_half_even
- DISC-001 can be marked FIXED in DISCREPANCIES.md","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-14T12:00:00Z","created_by":"claude","updated_at":"2026-04-14T14:18:00Z","closed_at":"2026-04-14T14:18:00Z","close_reason":"FIXED: Changed format_f and format_e in printf.rs to use round_ties_even() instead of round(). Added test_format_float_bankers_rounding test. Updated DISCREPANCIES.md.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","discrepancy","ieee754","printf"]}
 {"id":"bd-cfm2","title":"Scanf DISC-004: Implement hex float parsing (0x1.fp+2)","description":"Implement C11 hex float format parsing for scanf %a/%A specifiers. Currently hex floats like 0x1.fp+2 (= 7.75) fail to parse.

Format: 0x[h...h][.h...h][pN] where h is hex digit, N is decimal exponent.
Spec: C11 7.21.6.2 paragraph 12 describes %a/%A hex float format.","acceptance_criteria":"- sscanf("0x1.fp+2", "%a", &f) parses correctly to 7.75
- sscanf("0x1.0p10", "%a", &f) parses to 1024.0
- sscanf("0x1p0", "%a", &f) parses to 1.0
- All scanf hex float conformance tests pass
- DISC-004 can be marked FIXED in DISCREPANCIES.md","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-14T12:00:01Z","created_by":"claude","updated_at":"2026-04-14T14:20:00Z","closed_at":"2026-04-14T14:20:00Z","close_reason":"FIXED: Implemented scan_hex_float in scanf.rs to parse hex floats. Added 5 unit tests covering basic, integer, negative, negative exponent, and uppercase cases. Updated DISCREPANCIES.md.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","discrepancy","ieee754","scanf"]}
 {"id":"bd-cj0","title":"Syscall core: introduce raw Linux x86_64 syscall veneer (no libc::syscall)","description":"Critique mapping: #2.

Deliverables:
- Add a minimal raw syscall veneer module with typed wrappers for read/write/openat/close/mmap/munmap/mprotect/futex/clone/exit.
- No libc::syscall usage in replacement profile paths.
- Invariants doc: argument marshalling, errno handling, restart semantics.

Acceptance:
- Symbol-level tests confirm wrappers return glibc-compatible errno semantics.
- rg check proves replacement profile call graph contains zero libc::syscall invocations.

Evidence & Test Requirements:
- Add comprehensive unit tests (happy-path, negative, adversarial, and regression cases) for all changed logic.
- Add or update deterministic e2e scripts that exercise strict and hardened modes where relevant.
- Emit structured detailed logs for test/e2e runs (trace_id, mode, API/symbol, outcome, errno, timing, and artifact refs).
- Bead cannot close until test commands and log artifact locations are documented.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CrimsonCove","created_at":"2026-02-11T02:48:09.109281820Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T16:28:47.599408Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T16:28:47.599408Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["critique","p0-path","syscall"]}
+{"id":"bd-cl5u","title":"Conformance: add execute_* handlers for setjmp_ops family (setjmp, _setjmp, longjmp)","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T02:04:53.752782320Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T02:04:57.485345691Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","testing"]}
 {"id":"bd-cmiv","title":"EPIC: Implement real DNS resolution for getaddrinfo","description":"getaddrinfo() currently only resolves:
1. Numeric IP addresses (127.0.0.1, ::1)
2. Hostnames found in /etc/hosts

It does NOT perform actual DNS queries. This means programs like wget, curl (for non-localhost URLs), dig, nslookup, and any network client that resolves hostnames will fail.

OPTIONS:
A) Delegate to host libc's getaddrinfo for non-numeric, non-/etc/hosts hostnames
B) Implement a minimal DNS stub resolver (send UDP query to nameserver from /etc/resolv.conf)
C) Both: try our resolver first, fall back to host

Option A is fastest to implement and most reliable. Option B gives us full independence from host libc.

ALREADY DONE: /etc/hosts lookup works, numeric IP parsing works, freeaddrinfo correctly frees our Box-allocated structures.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:07:15.603558212Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-03T18:43:31.403160236Z","closed_at":"2026-04-03T18:43:31.402959135Z","close_reason":"Already implemented. getaddrinfo has a full native DNS stub resolver: udp_dns_query() sends A/AAAA queries to /etc/resolv.conf nameservers via SYS_socket/SYS_sendto/SYS_recvfrom with configurable timeout/retry. ResolverConfig parses nameservers, search domains, ndots. Resolution chain: numeric -> /etc/hosts -> DNS -> repair fallback. 46 resolv tests pass. Previously verified when closing bd-zh1y.7 and bd-1y7.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-cmiv.1","title":"Add host libc delegation for DNS-requiring getaddrinfo calls","description":"When getaddrinfo receives a hostname that:
1. Is NOT a numeric IP address
2. Is NOT found in /etc/hosts

Delegate to the host libc's getaddrinfo via our ELF resolver. The host's getaddrinfo will perform the actual DNS query.

IMPLEMENTATION:
1. resolve_host_symbol_raw("getaddrinfo") to get host's function pointer
2. Call host_getaddrinfo(node, service, hints, res) 
3. Return the host's result directly (the addrinfo structs are heap-allocated by the host)
4. In freeaddrinfo, detect whether the addrinfo was allocated by us (Box) or host (malloc) and free accordingly

TRICKY PART: freeaddrinfo must distinguish our Box-allocated addrinfos from host-malloc-allocated ones. Could use a sentinel field or check if the pointer is in our Box allocation range.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:07:15.811880807Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T03:30:57.450087855Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T03:30:57.448613314Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-cmiv.1","depends_on_id":"bd-cmiv","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:07:15.811880807Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-w2c3.9.1","title":"T8.1 Unit Test Closure Packs by Family","description":"## Background
Build exhaustive unit packs for remaining weak/complex families with adversarial and regression coverage.

## Design
Cover startup, loader edges, resolver/NSS, locale/iconv, signal/setjmp, SysV IPC, unwinding/session paths.

## Acceptance Criteria
All packs include strict/hardened cases where relevant and explicit expected invariants.

## Notes
Output per-pack coverage and failure diagnostics with trace IDs.","design":"Cover startup, loader edges, resolver/NSS, locale/iconv, signal/setjmp, SysV IPC, unwinding/session paths.","acceptance_criteria":"All packs include strict/hardened cases where relevant and explicit expected invariants.

### Mandatory Verification Gate
- Comprehensive unit tests are required for all touched logic: positive, negative, adversarial, and regression cases.
- Deterministic e2e scripts are required for all externally visible behavior in this bead (strict and hardened mode where relevant).
- Structured logs are mandatory for unit/e2e runs with fields: `trace_id`, `mode`, `api_family`, `symbol`, `decision_path`, `healing_action`, `errno`, `latency_ns`, `artifact_refs`.
- Closure evidence must include: exact test commands, expected outputs/thresholds, and representative failure signatures.
- CI must persist artifacts (logs, diffs, reports) with deterministic naming and retention policy for triage/replay.
- Tooling hard requirement applies: use `/dp/asupersync` for deterministic orchestration/traceability and `/dp/frankentui` for deterministic diff/snapshot/TUI analysis output.","notes":"Output per-pack coverage and failure diagnostics with trace IDs.

### Mandatory Test/E2E Logging Checklist
- Test command set documented in bead comments/notes before closure (unit + e2e + perf where applicable).
- Expected output and pass/fail thresholds documented per command.
- Failure triage snippets documented (top 3 likely failure classes + where to inspect artifacts).
- Evidence index entry documented for this bead linking logs, parity diffs, and benchmark/proof artifacts.
- Overload behavior logging required when applicable: pressure signal, degradation mode, selected policy, guard decision, rollback trigger.

Tooling contract: closure requires evidence of `/dp/asupersync` orchestration/traceability integration and `/dp/frankentui` deterministic diff/snapshot analysis integration.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:15:30.722377618Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T18:29:37.156901233Z","closed_at":"2026-02-13T18:29:37.156878831Z","close_reason":"Unit test closure packs complete: 7 new fixtures (49 cases), 12 required families covered, CI gate + 4 harness tests pass. 48 total fixtures, 470 cases.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["execution","feature-parity","gap-closure"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-w2c3.9.1","depends_on_id":"bd-w2c3.8.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:34Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-w2c3.9.1","depends_on_id":"bd-w2c3.9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:34Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1213,"issue_id":"bd-w2c3.9.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"CLAIMING: PearlFinch starting bd-w2c3.9.1. Will build unit test closure packs for remaining weak/complex families with strict/hardened coverage. bd-w2c3.8.1 is CLOSED, unblocked.","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:26:04Z"},{"id":1214,"issue_id":"bd-w2c3.9.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"DONE: Created 7 new closure pack fixtures (setjmp_ops, sysv_ipc_ops, backtrace_ops, session_ops, loader_edges, spawn_exec_ops, regex_glob_ops) totaling 49 new cases all with strict+hardened mode coverage. CI gate check_unit_test_packs.sh validates 12 required families. 4 Rust harness tests pass. Total: 48 fixture files, 470 cases, 64% symbol coverage. Closing. —PearlFinch","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:29:35Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-w2c3.9.2","title":"T8.2 Deterministic E2E Packs: smoke/stress/fault/stability/security","description":"## Background
Expand deterministic e2e packs with strict+hardened pair-runs, replay controls, and failure triage bundles.

## Design
Scenario manifest includes normal workloads, adversarial misuse, long-run stability, and overload paths.

## Acceptance Criteria
Pair-run comparator reports strict-vs-hardened divergence with bounded/expected rationale.

## Notes
Artifact bundle includes logs, diffs, metrics, crash bundles, and replay command lines.","design":"Scenario manifest includes normal workloads, adversarial misuse, long-run stability, and overload paths.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria
Pair-run comparator reports strict-vs-hardened divergence with bounded/expected rationale.

### Mandatory Verification Gate
- Comprehensive unit tests are required for all touched logic: positive, negative, adversarial, and regression cases.
- Deterministic e2e scripts are required for all externally visible behavior in this bead (strict and hardened mode where relevant).
- Structured logs are mandatory for unit/e2e runs with fields: `trace_id`, `mode`, `api_family`, `symbol`, `decision_path`, `healing_action`, `errno`, `latency_ns`, `artifact_refs`.
- Closure evidence must include: exact test commands, expected outputs/thresholds, and representative failure signatures.
- CI must persist artifacts (logs, diffs, reports) with deterministic naming and retention policy for triage/replay.
- Mandatory build-tooling contract enforced: deterministic conformance orchestration and traceability must use `/dp/asupersync`, and deterministic diff/snapshot/TUI evidence surfaces must use `/dp/frankentui`.","notes":"Artifact bundle includes logs, diffs, metrics, crash bundles, and replay command lines.

### Mandatory Test/E2E Logging Checklist
- Test command set documented in bead comments/notes before closure (unit + e2e + perf where applicable).
- Expected output and pass/fail thresholds documented per command.
- Failure triage snippets documented (top 3 likely failure classes + where to inspect artifacts).
- Evidence index entry documented for this bead linking logs, parity diffs, and benchmark/proof artifacts.
- Overload behavior logging required when applicable: pressure signal, degradation mode, selected policy, guard decision, rollback trigger.

Tooling contract: this bead cannot close without explicit evidence that `/dp/asupersync` is used for deterministic orchestration/traceability and `/dp/frankentui` is used for deterministic diff/snapshot-oriented analysis output.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:15:30.981800759Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-07T23:02:47.242268615Z","closed_at":"2026-04-07T23:02:47.242032247Z","close_reason":"Deliverables present in working tree from swarm + direct work.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["execution","feature-parity","gap-closure"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-w2c3.9.2","depends_on_id":"bd-w2c3.4.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:34Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-w2c3.9.2","depends_on_id":"bd-w2c3.4.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:34Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-w2c3.9.2","depends_on_id":"bd-w2c3.7.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:34Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-w2c3.9.2","depends_on_id":"bd-w2c3.8.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:34Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-w2c3.9.2","depends_on_id":"bd-w2c3.8.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:34Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-w2c3.9.2","depends_on_id":"bd-w2c3.9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:34Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-w2c3.9.2","depends_on_id":"bd-w2c3.9.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:34Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-w2c3.9.3","title":"T8.3 Structured Log/Evidence Unification + Artifact Index","description":"## Background
Unify runtime decision logs, evidence ledger records, conformance outputs, and e2e artifacts into one queryable index.

## Design
Schema guarantees join keys across trace_id/span_id/controller_id/policy_id/evidence_seqno.

## Acceptance Criteria
Index enables one-command triage from failing bead/gate to all relevant artifacts.

## Notes
Add schema-validation tests and backward-compatibility migration checks.","design":"Schema guarantees join keys across trace_id/span_id/controller_id/policy_id/evidence_seqno.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria
Index enables one-command triage from failing bead/gate to all relevant artifacts.

### Mandatory Verification Gate
- Comprehensive unit tests are required for all touched logic: positive, negative, adversarial, and regression cases.
- Deterministic e2e scripts are required for all externally visible behavior in this bead (strict and hardened mode where relevant).
- Structured logs are mandatory for unit/e2e runs with fields: `trace_id`, `mode`, `api_family`, `symbol`, `decision_path`, `healing_action`, `errno`, `latency_ns`, `artifact_refs`.
- Closure evidence must include: exact test commands, expected outputs/thresholds, and representative failure signatures.
- CI must persist artifacts (logs, diffs, reports) with deterministic naming and retention policy for triage/replay.
- Mandatory build-tooling contract enforced: deterministic conformance orchestration and traceability must use `/dp/asupersync`, and deterministic diff/snapshot/TUI evidence surfaces must use `/dp/frankentui`.","notes":"Add schema-validation tests and backward-compatibility migration checks.

- Failure triage snippets documented (top 3 likely failure classes + where to inspect artifacts).
- Evidence index entry documented for this bead linking logs, parity diffs, and benchmark/proof artifacts.
- Overload behavior logging required when applicable: pressure signal, degradation mode, selected policy, guard decision, rollback trigger.

Tooling contract: this bead cannot close without explicit evidence that `/dp/asupersync` is used for deterministic orchestration/traceability and `/dp/frankentui` is used for deterministic diff/snapshot-oriented analysis output.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:15:31.241516216Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-06T23:01:52.727948028Z","closed_at":"2026-04-06T23:01:52.727687585Z","close_reason":"Implemented shared artifact-index compatibility and schema/migration coverage","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["execution","feature-parity","gap-closure"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-w2c3.9.3","depends_on_id":"bd-w2c3.2.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:34Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-w2c3.9.3","depends_on_id":"bd-w2c3.7.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:34Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-w2c3.9.3","depends_on_id":"bd-w2c3.9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:34Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-w2c3.9.3","depends_on_id":"bd-w2c3.9.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:34Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-w2c3.9.3","depends_on_id":"bd-w2c3.9.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:34Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1394,"issue_id":"bd-w2c3.9.3","author":"OlivePuma","text":"Bounded partial-unblock slice landed in frankenlibc-harness shadow-run: added artifact-index output + canonical trace_id propagation for scenario reports/logs/artifacts, wired , and extended shadow-run tests to assert artifact-index join keys. Verification via rch: 
running 8 tests
test manifest_identifies_shadow_scenarios ... ok
test executor_writes_requested_artifacts ... ok
test nonzero_allowed_exit_code_does_not_trigger_baseline_error ... ok
test missing_required_artifact_fails_shadow_manifest ... ok
test smoke_fixture_placeholder_is_resolved_into_replay_command ... ok
test optional_missing_binary_is_skipped_instead_of_aborting_manifest ... ok
test expected_divergence_can_satisfy_shadow_scenario_contract ... ok
test run_shadow_manifest_writes_report_log_and_replay_bundle ... ok

test result: ok. 8 passed; 0 failed; 0 ignored; 0 measured; 0 filtered out; finished in 0.12s PASS;  PASS;  PASS. Remaining non-slice failures: workspace-wide Diff in /data/projects/frankenlibc/crates/frankenlibc-membrane/tests/ebr_integration_test.rs:359:
         d.total_reclaimed <= d.total_retired,
         "cannot reclaim more than retired"
     );
[31m-    assert!(d.global_epoch > 0, "mixed workload should advance the global epoch");
(B[m[32m+    assert!(
(B[m[32m+        d.global_epoch > 0,
(B[m[32m+        "mixed workload should advance the global epoch"
(B[m[32m+    );
(B[m     assert_eq!(
         d.pending_per_epoch.iter().sum::<usize>(),
         0, still fails on pre-existing formatting in ;  still fails on pre-existing dead_code in .","created_at":"2026-04-06T21:43:07Z"},{"id":1395,"issue_id":"bd-w2c3.9.3","author":"ubuntu","text":"Completed shared artifact-index compatibility + schema coverage on top of the shadow-run slice.

Changes:
- structured_log now accepts legacy artifact-index join-key aliases (`trace_id`, `span_id`, `decision_id`, `policy_id`, `evidence_seqno`) and normalizes them into canonical plural arrays.
- artifact indexes missing `run_id`/`generated_utc` now deserialize with deterministic legacy defaults via `normalize_legacy_defaults()`.
- evidence_compliance emits a WARN proof-log event (`evidence_compliance.artifact_index_legacy_defaults`) when legacy defaults are synthesized, instead of failing older v1 bundles.
- added schema/migration coverage in `structured_log_test` and `evidence_compliance_test`, including the canonical `log_schema.json` artifact-index example and a legacy index without `run_id`.

Verification:
- `rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_frankenlibc cargo test -p frankenlibc-harness --test structured_log_test --test evidence_compliance_test`
  Expected: 17 focused tests pass.
  Observed: PASS (6 evidence-compliance tests + 11 structured-log tests).
- `rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_frankenlibc cargo check --workspace --all-targets`
  Expected: workspace check passes.
  Observed: PASS.
- `rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_frankenlibc cargo fmt --check`
  Observed: FAIL due pre-existing formatting drift in `crates/frankenlibc-abi/build.rs` (not this slice).
- `rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_frankenlibc cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings`
  Observed: FAIL due pre-existing `clippy::manual_is_multiple_of` in `crates/frankenlibc-membrane/tests/alien_cs_e2e_test.rs:170` and `:173` (outside this slice).

Failure triage:
1. Legacy artifact-index bundles missing `run_id` should now produce a WARN proof-log event, not an `artifact_index.invalid_json` parse failure. Inspect `evidence_compliance.proof.log.jsonl`.
2. Singular legacy join-key fields should serialize back out as canonical plural arrays. Inspect `structured_log_test` roundtrip output if this regresses.
3. Remaining workspace fmt/clippy failures are unrelated repo drift; inspect the files above before blaming this bead.","created_at":"2026-04-06T23:01:40Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-wud","title":"Mode semantics matrix: strict vs hardened behavior table per API family","description":"Critique mapping: #2 + #5.

Deliverables:
- Machine-generated strict/hardened semantic delta table for each API family.
- Include which repairs are admissible and which are forbidden per mode.

Acceptance:
- Docs and harness consume same source table.
- No hidden mode-specific behavior without explicit table entry.

Evidence & Test Requirements:
- Add comprehensive unit tests (happy-path, negative, adversarial, and regression cases) for all changed logic.
- Add or update deterministic e2e scripts that exercise strict and hardened modes where relevant.
- Emit structured detailed logs for test/e2e runs (trace_id, mode, API/symbol, outcome, errno, timing, and artifact refs).
- Bead cannot close until test commands and log artifact locations are documented.","notes":"2026-02-11 closure (CrimsonCove): Updated mode_semantics_matrix.json (Threading heals=0, Stdlib heals=10, added getenv/setenv/unsetenv). Cascading fixes applied to replacement_profile.json (io_abi census=0, stdlib_abi added), verification_matrix.json (bd-182/bd-2bu/bd-3tc evidence), and perf_budget_policy.json.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["critique","docs","semantics"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-wud","depends_on_id":"bd-2bu","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:34Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-wuh","title":"EPIC: RaptorQ-inspired runtime resilience (erasure-coded evidence + metadata self-healing)","description":"Goal: use RaptorQ patterns accretively inside libc runtime, without adding /dp/asupersync as a runtime dependency.

Core idea (pattern, not copy-paste):
- Systematic fast path: store source symbols (raw evidence) cheaply.
- Repair path: generate deterministic repair symbols on a cadence (epoch boundary), not per call.
- Decode + proof: decoding and explainability happen in diagnostics/harness, not on the strict hot path.

Candidate uses:
1) Erasure-coded evidence ring buffer for runtime_math decisions/heals/anomalies.
2) Self-healing for small critical metadata capsules (headers/roots), not entire large tables.
3) Cross-shard summary reconstruction to support cohomology/consistency monitors under partial loss.

Success criteria:
- Strict fast path unchanged; hardened adds bounded overhead only when enabled.
- Deterministic encoding parameters + evidence-ledger tuning of redundancy.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-09T21:30:15.573551832Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T03:03:13.254498204Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T03:03:13.145127019Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-wuh","depends_on_id":"bd-284","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:34Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-wuh","depends_on_id":"bd-2xr","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:34Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-wuh","depends_on_id":"bd-3a9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:34Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-wuh","depends_on_id":"bd-3ku","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:34Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-wuh","depends_on_id":"bd-pc4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:34Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1215,"issue_id":"bd-wuh","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Epic Notes: RaptorQ-Inspired Resilience Inside libc Runtime

### North Star
Turn fragile “best effort” telemetry into **recoverable, explainable evidence** under partial loss, while keeping libc hot paths fast.

RaptorQ is the reference pattern because it gives us:
- systematic fast path (zero overhead when nothing is lost)
- deterministic repair generation (no coordination required)
- recovery from erasures (dropped/missing records) with small overhead
- explainability via decode proofs (in tooling)

### Non-Negotiable Constraints
- No `/dp/asupersync` in the runtime dependency graph.
- Strict mode hot path stays “boring”: constant-time, no allocations, no locks.
- Hardened mode overhead is bounded and measured (microbench + regression gates).

### Concrete libc Use Cases (Scope)
1. **Erasure-coded evidence ring**
   - Evidence includes: runtime_math decision + key context + membrane outcome + heal action + stage exits.
   - Goal: if we drop N% of events or overwrite parts of the ring, tooling can still reconstruct a consistent prefix / windows.
2. **Self-healing critical metadata capsules**
   - Small, high-value state snapshots (kernel snapshot schema, policy tables hash, config epoch) should survive partial corruption.
3. **Cross-shard reconstruction for consistency monitors**
   - Cohomology/consistency monitors can treat “missing overlap witnesses” as erasures and reconstruct summaries.

### Runtime Design Principles (Extreme Optimization)
- Evidence recording per call:
  - `seq = atomic_fetch_add(Relaxed)`
  - store a fixed-size record into a per-thread ring slot
  - no heap alloc
  - no syscalls
  - no GF(256) operations
- Repair symbol generation:
  - done on cadence: every `K_source` events, per-thread or global epoch
  - uses deterministic seed; work amortized
  - may run in a background worker thread only in hardened mode
- Export:
  - tooling/harness reads the ring (or a frozen snapshot) and performs decode + proof.

### Reliability Model
We model losses as:
- erasures: records not present (ring overwrite, sampling drop, partial capture)
- corruption: payload hash mismatch (bit flips, races)

We treat corruption as “erase and try to repair” (don’t let bad bytes poison decode).

### Adaptive Redundancy (Control)
- Observe: loss rate (erasures) and corruption rate in exported traces.
- Decide: raise redundancy when anytime-valid evidence says we’re violating durability/observability budgets.
- Act: increase `R` (repair symbols) in future epochs; optionally retro-harden recent epochs.

This is implemented via `bd-284` and wired to runtime_math e-process + evidence ledger.

### Deliverables / Done Means
- Encoding path exists with strict-mode overhead indistinguishable from baseline.
- Offline decode exists and verifies record integrity + hash-chain.
- Loss/corruption simulation tests demonstrate reconstruction up to documented budgets.
- Evidence reports are explainable: when repair happens, tooling emits a DecodeProof.","created_at":"2026-02-09T21:50:51Z"},{"id":1216,"issue_id":"bd-wuh","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"EPIC COMPLETE. All 5 blockers resolved: bd-3a9 (architecture), bd-pc4 (offline decoder), bd-2xr (loss simulation), bd-3ku (evidence integration), bd-284 (adaptive tuner). Evidence ring buffer integrated, adaptive redundancy tuner with e-process + audit ledger, 69 modules, all quality gates passing.","created_at":"2026-02-11T03:03:13Z"}]}
